journal_entry.description: Purchase of tea, beverages, and refreshments for employee break room
journal_entry.rationale: Tea and beverages for employee break room are operating expenses. Basic groceries (tea, beverages) are tax-exempt in Canada, but the 10% tax suggests non-basic items or prepared beverages were included. Since the transaction states 10% tax was charged (not the 13% HST rate for ON), this appears to be a mixed basket where some items may be taxable. The corporate credit card creates a liability (credit card payable). Given the stated tax amount, we record the expense at $1,200, a separate tax receivable for the $120 GST portion that can be recovered (assuming the 10% represents recoverable input tax), and increase credit card payable by the total $1,320.

output_debit_classifier.reason: Employee break room refreshments are an operating expense. The 10% tax charged (totaling $120) is recorded as HST Receivable since it represents recoverable input tax on business purchases. Credit card payment creates a liability. Debit tuple: 1 asset increase (HST Receivable) + 1 expense increase (refreshments). Credit tuple: 1 liability increase (credit card payable).
